meta.title: Per-principle schema — extracted from taxonomy.json
meta.description: The schema every principle in principles.json conforms to. Extracted from taxonomy.json on 2026-06-01 so the schema can be consumed standalone without the enums / usage / conventions noise. Source of truth remains taxonomy.json; this file is a derived view.

principle_schema.intro: Every principle in principles.json conforms to this schema. This is what makes the catalogue different from cloud-vendor frameworks: each principle names who validates it, when it is checked, and what artefacts the validator inspects. Vendor frameworks stop at the question; this catalogue defines the audit.
principle_schema.vendor_neutrality_note: The schema is vendor-neutral. Pillar names match AWS / Azure / GCP WAF terminology because those names are de-facto industry-standard. The catalogue's primary anchor framework is the AWS Well-Architected Generative AI Lens, but the framework_mappings field is structured to hold parallel mappings to Azure, GCP, NIST, OWASP, or any other framework without schema change.

fields.name: standard_id
fields.description: The node's own stable identifier. As of schema v1.13 (2026-06-09) each node is a STANDARD, identified `ST-<bp_code>-NN` (e.g. ST-GO1B1-01). `bp_code` is a 5-character compression of the AWS pillar / question / BP triple and `NN` is a two-digit sequential counter, consecutive within a BP with no gaps. See conventions.standard_id_format in taxonomy.json.

fields.name: principle_id
fields.description: The principle this standard ladders up to: `PR-<bp_code>-NN` (e.g. PR-GO1B1-01). One principle may carry several standards (one-to-many), so multiple nodes can share a `principle_id`; the mapping is currently 1:1. Re-prefixed from the bare `<bp_code>-NN` form in schema v1.13 (2026-06-09). NOTE: dependencies[].principle_id and the data/ri/<id>/ folder names still use the bare legacy id pending a reconciliation pass. See conventions.principle_id_format in taxonomy.json.

fields.name: u_value
fields.description: The short value name this principle ladders up to — e.g. Fairness, Safety, Privacy, Confidentiality, Accountable Agency, Responsible Stewardship. The aspirational, human-facing label sitting above the rule; multiple principles may share one value. Added schema v1.12 (2026-06-09).

fields.name: u_principle
fields.description: The aspirational, why-first principle statement — a durable value expressed as the reason the rule exists, stated WITHOUT naming any artefact, gate, or enforcement mechanism (e.g. 'Acting in the world is irreversible, so nothing consequential happens on the model's say-so alone'). Sits ABOVE `statement` (the abstract rule) and `gates` (the standard / enforcement). Added schema v1.12 (2026-06-09).

fields.name: pillar
fields.description: One of P1–P5. Maps to a GenAI Lens pillar. Pillar names are identical across AWS, Azure, and GCP WAFs.

fields.name: focus_area
fields.description: Focus area code. Internal taxonomy slot.

fields.name: impact_level
fields.description: Critical / High / Medium / Low. Drives criticality-tier applicability.

fields.name: applicability
fields.description: Object mapping AI system patterns (llm / rag / agentic / ml) to the principle's criticality for each pattern (mandatory / nice_to_have). Omit patterns where the principle does not materially apply — absence means 'not applicable to this pattern'.

fields.name: serving_paradigm
fields.description: Object mapping foundation-model serving paradigms (api_on_demand / api_provisioned / self_hosted_managed / self_hosted_unmanaged) to the principle's criticality for each paradigm. Orthogonal to applicability. Omit paradigms where the principle does not materially apply.

fields.name: maturity_level
fields.description: Organisational maturity at which this principle should be adopted: foundational (first AI project), scaling (2–5 projects or multi-team), mature (5+ project portfolio with formal ARB / dedicated platform team). Pick the strictest level that honestly applies.

fields.name: dependencies
fields.item_structure: { principle_id: string, kind: string, reason: string }
fields.description: Other catalogue principles this principle DIRECTLY depends on — the substrate principles whose artefacts or enforcement machinery this principle's gates build on or reuse. Each entry: `principle_id` (the depended-on principle), `kind` (enums.dependency_kind: 'hard' — this principle cannot be enforced or cannot function without the dependency's substrate in place; 'soft' — it composes with / is strengthened by the dependency but can be adopted without it), and `reason` (one sentence naming the concrete artefact or mechanism inherited). Empty array `[]` for foundational principles that stand alone. List only DIRECT dependencies, not the transitive closure. See conventions.dependencies. Added schema v1.10 (2026-06-05).

fields.name: references
fields.item_structure: { url: string, title: string, source: string, note: string, date: string }
fields.description: Real-world evidence that this principle's failure mode occurs in industry: published incident write-ups, engineering postmortems, vendor case studies, or practitioner accounts documenting the problem the principle prevents. Each entry: `url` (link to a public page), `title` (hyperlink display text), `source` (publisher/author, e.g. 'Adaline', 'ProjectDiscovery'), `note` (one sentence on what the link shows and how it maps to this principle's failure mode), `date` (YYYY-MM-DD captured). Distinct from `problem.examples`, which are synthetic in-house scenarios — references are external, real, and citable. Empty array `[]` when no real-world write-up has yet been mapped to this principle. Surfaced as hyperlinks in the runtime UI. Added schema v1.11 (2026-06-06). See conventions.references and references_spec.

fields.name: framework_mappings
fields.description: External-framework anchors keyed by framework (`aws`, `azure`, `gcp`, `aigp`, …); each framework holds a `references` array. See principle_schema.framework_mappings_spec for the full sub-schema.

fields.name: ownership
fields.description: Who validates the principle and how. See principle_schema.ownership_spec for tiers and the tier rubric reference.

fields.name: statement
fields.description: The abstract rule — the durable 'what must hold', written at should/ought altitude without naming the enforcing artefact (that lives in `gates`). As of the 2026-06-09 altitude split this is no longer the aspirational layer: the value name and aspirational why-statement live in `u_value` / `u_principle` above it, and the concrete enforcement lives in `gates` below it.

fields.name: problem
fields.description: The failure mode the principle prevents, with concrete examples.

fields.name: solution
fields.description: How to implement, what good looks like.

fields.name: gates
fields.item_structure: { point: string, check: string, blocking: boolean }
fields.description: When the principle is checked in the lifecycle and what binary check fires at each point. See principle_schema.gates_spec for lifecycle points and the required-status-check constraint.

fields.name: evidence
fields.structure: { artefacts: array, review_mode: string, sign_off: string }
fields.description: What the central validator clicks on at a central review gate. Required when audit_mode is central_review_at_gate; may be blank otherwise. See principle_schema.evidence_spec.

fields.name: change_history
fields.description: Versioned audit trail of edits to this principle. See principle_schema.change_history_spec.

fields.name: explain_prompt
fields.structure: { system: string, user_template: string, compiled_for_principle_version: string, compiled_at: string, compiled_by: string }
fields.description: Optional compiled OpenAI-style prompt pair used by the runtime UI's 'Explain' affordance. Absent when the principle has not yet been compiled.

framework_mappings_spec.intro: Each principle's relationship to external frameworks is captured in the framework_mappings field. The field is an object keyed by framework identifier; each framework key holds a references array of zero or more reference objects.
framework_mappings_spec.primary_anchor: AWS is the catalogue's primary anchor: every principle must concretise (or explicitly extend with mapping_state: na) an AWS GenAI Lens BP. Other frameworks (AIGP, NIST, etc.) are cross-references — informational only.
framework_mappings_spec.anchoring_default: implementation_step — principles concretise specific AWS implementation steps, not whole BPs.

common_reference_fields.name: note
common_reference_fields.description: Free-text rationale: what our principle adds beyond the framework's guidance.

aws.name: implementation_step
aws.structure: { number: integer, title: string, verbatim_text: string | null }

ownership_spec.intro: Two tiers via `ownership.tier`. Tier reflects CENTRALISATION OF ENFORCEMENT INFRASTRUCTURE, not validation. Tier is independent of `validator` and `audit_mode`.

tiers.project: Each project team builds its own enforcement tooling. PA self-attests using locally-built tooling.
tiers.enterprise: The platform team owns the enforcement infrastructure. Project teams consume the platform team's tooling rather than rebuilding.

ownership_spec.tier_rubric_ref: agentflow/sections/tier/rubric.json — D1 (legal_exposure, binary veto) + D2 (repeatability_cost, 0–3). Outcomes: mandatory_centralise / recommended_centralise → enterprise; local → project.

ownership_object_fields.name: audit_mode
ownership_object_fields.description: self_attestation_with_mechanical_evidence | central_review_at_gate. Drives whether evidence artefacts are required.

ownership_object_fields.name: arb_role
ownership_object_fields.description: What ARB's role is (e.g. dashboard_and_spot_check, primary_reviewer).

gates_spec.intro: Each gate is a { point, check, blocking } triple. point is the lifecycle moment the check fires. check is a binary statement. blocking indicates whether failure prevents progression.
gates_spec.required_status_check_constraint: For CI-enforced gates, the check field must explicitly state that the check is configured as a REQUIRED status check on the integration branch — not advisory.

evidence_spec.intro: Evidence enumerates artefacts a central validator clicks on at a central review gate. Required when ownership.audit_mode is 'central_review_at_gate'; may be blank otherwise.

shape.sign_off: binary (pass or fail; no graded scale)

change_history_spec.intro: Every principle carries a change_history field tracking its evolution over time. Append-only.
change_history_spec.shape: { current_version: string, changes: [{ version, date, author, summary }] }
change_history_spec.version_rules: MAJOR for rule changes, MINOR for additions, PATCH for clarifications. current_version mirrors the most recent entry in changes.

references_spec.intro: The references field holds external, real-world evidence that the principle's failure mode is not hypothetical — published incidents, engineering postmortems, vendor case studies, practitioner write-ups. It is the citable counterpart to problem.examples (which are synthetic, in-house illustrations): examples teach the failure shape, references prove it happens.

entry_fields.name: url
entry_fields.description: The link. Must resolve to a public page.

entry_fields.name: title
entry_fields.description: Hyperlink display text — the article/page title or a short descriptive label.

entry_fields.name: note
entry_fields.description: One sentence: what the link demonstrates and how it maps to this principle's failure mode.

references_spec.authoring_rule: Only add a reference when the linked material genuinely documents THIS principle's failure mode — not generic topic-adjacent content. Empty array [] is correct and expected until matching evidence is found.
references_spec.distinct_from_problem_examples: problem.examples are synthetic scenarios authored to illustrate the failure shape; references are external, real, and citable.
